panels.title: Worker Heartbeat Staleness
panels.description: Seconds since each worker last updated `crawler_worker_heartbeat_timestamp_seconds`. Healthy workers tick every few seconds. >600s fires the WorkerStuck alert (page) — process is alive but the claim/dispatch loop has wedged (Playwright deadlock, scraper hang, etc.). See #3169.

panels.title: Cron Job Last Run
panels.description: Per-cron last-run telemetry from `crawler_cron_last_run_ts` and `crawler_cron_last_run_status` (#2704). >6h since last run fires the CronJobMissing alert (page); `status=0` fires CronJobFailing (email). Requires CRAWLER_PUSHGATEWAY_URL to be set in the cron's runtime env; table is empty until then. See #3167.

panels.title: Task Throughput — HTTP
panels.description: Monitors and scrapes completed per minute by HTTP workers (worker-1/2/3). Green=monitor success, blue=scrape success, red/orange=failures.

panels.title: Success Rate — HTTP
panels.description: Percentage of tasks that succeed across all HTTP workers. Healthy: >95%.

panels.title: Jobs Discovered — HTTP
panels.description: New, relisted, and gone job postings detected per minute by HTTP monitors. Spikes when boards with many listings are checked.

panels.title: Task Throughput — Browser
panels.description: Same as HTTP but for browser worker (Playwright/Chromium). Lower throughput is expected — each task launches a headless browser.

panels.title: Success Rate — Browser
panels.description: Percentage of tasks that succeed on the browser worker. ~88% is normal — some sites block or timeout.

panels.title: Jobs Discovered — Browser
panels.description: Same as HTTP but for browser-dependent monitors (api_sniffer, dom with render=true).

panels.title: Queue Depth — HTTP due now
panels.description: Domains with work ready to claim right now per tier. T0=first-time, T1=monitors due, T2=scrapes due. Should stay near 0 if workers keep up.

panels.title: Total Domains — HTTP
panels.description: All domains in each tier including future-scheduled. Shows the full queue size — work arrives as boards come due on their check_interval.

panels.title: Queue Depth — Browser due now
panels.description: Browser domains with work ready now. Drains slower than HTTP due to Chromium overhead.

panels.title: Total Domains — Browser
panels.description: All browser domains across tiers. ~80 domains total for browser-dependent boards.

panels.title: Dead-letter Depth
panels.description: Tasks parked in Redis deadletter:<wtype> after the inflight reaper reaches max strikes. Nonzero for >1h fires DeadletterQueueNotEmpty. Inspect with ZRANGE deadletter:simple 0 -1 WITHSCORES or ZRANGE deadletter:browser 0 -1 WITHSCORES.

panels.title: Monitor Duration — HTTP
panels.description: p50/p95/p99 latency for HTTP monitor cycles. Most complete in 1-5s. Workday boards can take 30-60s (streaming thousands of jobs).

panels.title: Monitor Duration — Browser
panels.description: p50/p95/p99 for browser monitors. Higher baseline (10-30s) due to Playwright page load + rendering.

panels.title: Scrape Duration — HTTP
panels.description: p50/p95/p99 for individual job page scrapes via HTTP. Typically 1-5s per page.

panels.title: Scrape Duration — Browser
panels.description: p50/p95/p99 for rendered scrapes. 5-10s per page including Chromium startup.

panels.title: R2 Pending
panels.description: Descriptions waiting to be uploaded to R2. Should be near 0 after initial backlog clears. Only increases when description content actually changes (hash-based check).

panels.title: Exporter Staleness
panels.description: Seconds since last successful export tick. Should be <5s. >30s = exporter stuck or crashed.

panels.title: Export Flush Duration
panels.description: How long each exporter tick takes (p50/p95). <1s at steady state. High values during backlog catchup.

panels.title: R2 Upload Rate
panels.description: R2 PUT operations per second by the drain worker. ~40-50/s at peak. Should drop to near 0 at steady state.

panels.title: Exporter Rows/sec
panels.description: Rows exported to Supabase per second. High during backlog, near 0 at steady state thanks to conditional updated_at.

panels.title: Local Postgres Pool
panels.description: Connection pool for local Postgres (Hetzner). Size=total connections, Idle=unused, In Use=active queries.

panels.title: Container CPU
panels.description: Per-container CPU usage. Each HTTP worker should be near 100% (1 core). Browser worker uses up to 300% (3 cores).

panels.title: Container Memory
panels.description: Per-container RSS memory. HTTP workers ~150-300MB. Browser worker 1-2GB (Chromium). Watch for growth patterns indicating leaks.

panels.title: Disk Available
panels.description: Free disk space on root (/) and data mount (/mnt). Postgres data grows over time.

panels.title: Network I/O
panels.description: Machine network throughput. Receive=crawled pages from career sites. Transmit=R2 uploads + Supabase exports.
